terms.definition: A rapid, discontinuous change in the macro-state of a system when a control parameter crosses a critical threshold
The degree to which parts of a social system hold together in mutually reinforcing patterns
Aggregate external forces—legal, economic, cultural—that limit behavioural options
The threshold level of external constraint below which social order experiences a phase transition into lower coherence
The statistical tendency of a system to move toward higher-probability, less-ordered states
The removal of a causal linkage that previously constrained behaviour
The proportion of meaningful information (signal) to background distraction (noise) in an information channel
Producing a path-dependent obligation that narrows future options
A property wherein earlier institutional choices constrain current and future options
An environmental cost gradient that enforces behaviour independent of personal will
